Farm.title: Farm
Farm.description: Represents a single farm managed by the SmartPig application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Farm entity.

description.description: A brief description of the farm.

currency.description: The primary currency used for financial transactions on this farm (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR').

User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user account with access to the SmartPig application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

email.description: The user's email address, used for login.

role.description: The user's role, defining their permissions within the application.

farmId.description: Reference to the primary Farm this user is associated with. (Relationship: Farm 1:N User)

Sow.title: Sow
Sow.description: Represents an individual female pig managed for breeding purposes.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Sow entity.

farmId.description: Reference to the Farm this sow belongs to. (Relationship: Farm 1:N Sow)

earTag.description: The unique ear tag or identification number for the sow within the farm.

birthDate.description: The date of birth of the sow.

status.description: The current reproductive or general status of the sow (e.g., gestating, lactating, open, culled, died).

parity.description: The number of times the sow has farrowed (given birth).

entryDate.description: The date the sow entered the farm or the management system.

exitDate.description: The date the sow left the farm (e.g., culled, sold, died).

reasonForExit.description: The reason for the sow's departure from the farm (e.g., culling, sale, death).

notes.description: General notes or comments about the sow.

Boar.title: Boar
Boar.description: Represents an individual male pig managed for breeding purposes.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Boar entity.

farmId.description: Reference to the Farm this boar belongs to. (Relationship: Farm 1:N Boar)

earTag.description: The unique ear tag or identification number for the boar within the farm.

birthDate.description: The date of birth of the boar.

status.description: The current breeding or general status of the boar (e.g., active, retired, culled, died).

entryDate.description: The date the boar entered the farm or the management system.

exitDate.description: The date the boar left the farm (e.g., culled, sold, died).

reasonForExit.description: The reason for the boar's departure from the farm (e.g., culling, sale, death).

notes.description: General notes or comments about the boar.

Pig.title: Pig
Pig.description: Represents a general pig, used for tracking individual piglets, growing pigs, or finisher pigs.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Pig entity.

farmId.description: Reference to the Farm this pig belongs to. (Relationship: Farm 1:N Pig)

earTag.description: Optional unique ear tag or identification number for the pig. Only used for individual tracking.

birthDate.description: The date of birth of the pig.

sex.description: The sex of the pig (e.g., male, female, castrated).

currentWeightKg.description: The most recently recorded weight of the pig in kilograms.

stage.description: The current life stage of the pig (e.g., piglet, nursery, grower, finisher).

status.description: The current status of the pig (e.g., active, sold, died, culled, transferred).

currentBatchId.description: Reference to the Batch this pig is currently part of. (Relationship: Batch 1:N Pig)

currentFacilityId.description: Reference to the Facility where the pig is currently housed. (Relationship: Facility 1:N Pig)

sowMotherId.description: Reference to the Sow that is the mother of this pig. (Relationship: Sow 1:N Pig)

boarFatherId.description: Reference to the Boar that is the father of this pig. (Relationship: Boar 1:N Pig)

entryDate.description: The date the pig entered its current stage or batch.

exitDate.description: The date the pig left its current stage/batch or the farm (e.g., sold, died, transferred).

reasonForExit.description: The reason for the pig's departure from the farm or current stage.

notes.description: General notes or comments about the pig.

Batch.title: Batch
Batch.description: Represents a group of pigs managed together for tracking growth and finishing stages.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Batch entity.

farmId.description: Reference to the Farm this batch belongs to. (Relationship: Farm 1:N Batch)

name.description: A descriptive name for the batch (e.g., 'Batch 2023-Q4-Nursery').

stage.description: The current life stage of the pigs in the batch (e.g., nursery, grower, finisher, weaned, mixed).

startDate.description: The date the batch was started or formed.

endDate.description: The date the batch was completed, sold, or dissolved.

startQuantity.description: The initial number of pigs in the batch.

currentQuantity.description: The current number of pigs in the batch.

initialAverageWeightKg.description: The average weight of pigs in the batch when it started, in kilograms.

currentAverageWeightKg.description: The most recently recorded average weight of pigs in the batch, in kilograms.

facilityId.description: Reference to the Facility where the batch is currently housed. (Relationship: Facility 1:N Batch)

notes.description: General notes or comments about the batch.

Facility.title: Facility
Facility.description: Represents a physical location or area within the farm, such as a barn, room, or pen.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Facility entity.

farmId.description: Reference to the Farm this facility belongs to. (Relationship: Farm 1:N Facility)

name.description: The name of the facility (e.g., 'Barn 1', 'Maternity Room A', 'Pen 10').

type.description: The type of facility (e.g., barn, maternity, nursery, grower, finisher, quarantine, boar pen).

capacity.description: The maximum capacity of animals this facility can hold.

status.description: The current status of the facility (e.g., occupied, vacant, under cleaning, maintenance).

lastCleanedDate.description: The date when the facility was last cleaned and disinfected.

notes.description: General notes or comments about the facility.

MatingEvent.title: MatingEvent
MatingEvent.description: Records an event where a sow is serviced or mated.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MatingEvent entity.

farmId.description: Reference to the Farm where this event occurred. (Relationship: Farm 1:N MatingEvent)

sowId.description: Reference to the Sow involved in the mating event. (Relationship: Sow 1:N MatingEvent)

matingDate.description: The date and time of the mating event.

type.description: The type of mating (e.g., natural, AI - Artificial Insemination).

boarId.description: Reference to the Boar involved in the mating event, if natural mating. (Relationship: Boar 1:N MatingEvent)

semenBatchInventoryItemId.description: Reference to the InventoryItem representing the semen batch used, if AI. (Relationship: InventoryItem 1:N MatingEvent)

technicianUserId.description: Reference to the User who performed the mating event. (Relationship: User 1:N MatingEvent)

pregnancyConfirmed.description: Indicates if pregnancy was confirmed after the mating event.

pregnancyConfirmationDate.description: The date when pregnancy was confirmed.

expectedFarrowingDate.description: The calculated expected date for farrowing.

notes.description: General notes or comments about the mating event.

FarrowingEvent.title: FarrowingEvent
FarrowingEvent.description: Records details of a sow's farrowing (birth) event.

id.description: Unique identifier for the FarrowingEvent entity.

farmId.description: Reference to the Farm where this event occurred. (Relationship: Farm 1:N FarrowingEvent)

sowId.description: Reference to the Sow that farrowed. (Relationship: Sow 1:N FarrowingEvent)

matingEventId.description: Reference to the MatingEvent that resulted in this farrowing. (Relationship: MatingEvent 1:N FarrowingEvent)

farrowingDate.description: The date and time of the farrowing event.

liveBorn.description: The number of piglets born alive.

stillBorn.description: The number of piglets born dead.

mummified.description: The number of mummified fetuses born.

totalBorn.description: The total number of piglets born (live, stillborn, mummified).

birthWeightAverageKg.description: The average birth weight of the live-born piglets in kilograms.

litterQualityScore.description: A score indicating the overall quality of the litter (e.g., based on uniformity, vitality).

attendedByUserId.description: Reference to the User who attended or recorded the farrowing event. (Relationship: User 1:N FarrowingEvent)

notes.description: General notes or comments about the farrowing event or litter.

WeaningEvent.title: WeaningEvent
WeaningEvent.description: Records details of a weaning event, when piglets are separated from the sow.

id.description: Unique identifier for the WeaningEvent entity.

farmId.description: Reference to the Farm where this event occurred. (Relationship: Farm 1:N WeaningEvent)

sowId.description: Reference to the Sow from which the piglets were weaned. (Relationship: Sow 1:N WeaningEvent)

farrowingEventId.description: Reference to the FarrowingEvent that produced the piglets being weaned. (Relationship: FarrowingEvent 1:N WeaningEvent)

weaningDate.description: The date and time of the weaning event.

weanedQuantity.description: The number of piglets successfully weaned.

weaningAverageWeightKg.description: The average weight of the weaned piglets in kilograms.

destinationBatchId.description: Reference to the Batch where the weaned piglets were moved. (Relationship: Batch 1:N WeaningEvent)

destinationFacilityId.description: Reference to the Facility where the weaned piglets were moved. (Relationship: Facility 1:N WeaningEvent)

performedByUserId.description: Reference to the User who performed or recorded the weaning event. (Relationship: User 1:N WeaningEvent)

notes.description: General notes or comments about the weaning event.

HealthEvent.title: HealthEvent
HealthEvent.description: Records a health-related event for an animal or group, such as vaccination, treatment, or illness.

id.description: Unique identifier for the HealthEvent entity.

farmId.description: Reference to the Farm where this event occurred. (Relationship: Farm 1:N HealthEvent)

entityType.description: The type of entity this health event applies to (e.g., pig, sow, boar, batch).

entityId.description: Reference to the specific entity (Sow, Boar, Pig, or Batch) this event applies to. (Relationship: Generic Entity 1:N HealthEvent)

eventType.description: The specific type of health event (e.g., vaccination, treatment, illness, diagnosis, protocol application).

eventDate.description: The date and time the health event occurred.

medicationInventoryItemId.description: Reference to the InventoryItem representing the medication used, if applicable. (Relationship: InventoryItem 1:N HealthEvent)

diagnosis.description: The diagnosis for an illness event.

treatmentDescription.description: A description of the treatment administered.

performedByUserId.description: Reference to the User who performed or recorded the health event. (Relationship: User 1:N HealthEvent)

notes.description: General notes or comments about the health event.

FeedingEvent.title: FeedingEvent
FeedingEvent.description: Records an instance of feed consumption for an animal or group.

id.description: Unique identifier for the FeedingEvent entity.

farmId.description: Reference to the Farm where this event occurred. (Relationship: Farm 1:N FeedingEvent)

entityType.description: The type of entity this feeding event applies to (e.g., pig, sow, boar, batch).

entityId.description: Reference to the specific entity (Sow, Boar, Pig, or Batch) this event applies to. (Relationship: Generic Entity 1:N FeedingEvent)

feedingDate.description: The date and time the feed was consumed.

feedTypeId.description: Reference to the FeedType that was consumed. (Relationship: FeedType 1:N FeedingEvent)

quantityKg.description: The quantity of feed consumed in kilograms.

notes.description: General notes or comments about the feeding event.

recordedByUserId.description: Reference to the User who recorded the feeding event. (Relationship: User 1:N FeedingEvent)

FeedType.title: FeedType
FeedType.description: Defines different types of feed used on the farm.

id.description: Unique identifier for the FeedType entity.

farmId.description: Reference to the Farm this feed type is available at. (Relationship: Farm 1:N FeedType)

name.description: The name of the feed type (e.g., 'Pre-starter', 'Grower 1', 'Lactation Feed').

stage.description: The life stage of pigs for which this feed type is intended (e.g., pre-starter, starter, growth, finisher, lactation, gestation, boar).

description.description: A detailed description of the feed type, including ingredients or specific uses.

proteinContentPercentage.description: The protein content percentage of the feed.

energyContentKcalKg.description: The energy content of the feed in Kcal per kilogram.

costPerKg.description: The cost of this feed type per kilogram.

notes.description: General notes or comments about the feed type.

WeightEvent.title: WeightEvent
WeightEvent.description: Records a weight measurement for an individual animal or a batch.

id.description: Unique identifier for the WeightEvent entity.

farmId.description: Reference to the Farm where this event occurred. (Relationship: Farm 1:N WeightEvent)

entityType.description: The type of entity this weight event applies to (e.g., pig, sow, boar, batch).

entityId.description: Reference to the specific entity (Sow, Boar, Pig, or Batch) this event applies to. (Relationship: Generic Entity 1:N WeightEvent)

measurementDate.description: The date and time the weight was measured.

notes.description: General notes or comments about the weight measurement.

recordedByUserId.description: Reference to the User who recorded the weight event. (Relationship: User 1:N WeightEvent)

MovementEvent.title: MovementEvent
MovementEvent.description: Records the transfer, sale, death, or culling of an animal or batch.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MovementEvent entity.

farmId.description: Reference to the Farm where this event occurred. (Relationship: Farm 1:N MovementEvent)

entityType.description: The type of entity this movement event applies to (e.g., pig, sow, boar, batch).

entityId.description: Reference to the specific entity (Sow, Boar, Pig, or Batch) that moved. (Relationship: Generic Entity 1:N MovementEvent)

movementDate.description: The date and time of the movement event.

type.description: The type of movement (e.g., transfer, sale, death, cull, birth).

fromFacilityId.description: Reference to the Facility from which the entity moved. (Relationship: Facility 1:N MovementEvent)

toFacilityId.description: Reference to the Facility to which the entity moved. (Relationship: Facility 1:N MovementEvent)

destinationDetails.description: Details about the external destination if applicable (e.g., 'slaughterhouse XYZ', 'another farm ABC').

reason.description: The reason for the movement (e.g., 'illness', 'market ready', 'old age', 'accident').

quantity.description: The number of animals moved in this event (especially for batch movements).

pricePerKg.description: The price per kilogram if the movement was a sale.

totalRevenue.description: The total revenue generated if the movement was a sale.

notes.description: General notes or comments about the movement event.

recordedByUserId.description: Reference to the User who recorded the movement event. (Relationship: User 1:N MovementEvent)

InventoryItem.title: InventoryItem
InventoryItem.description: Tracks stock levels and details for various consumables and items on the farm (e.g., medications, semen, supplies).

id.description: Unique identifier for the InventoryItem entity.

farmId.description: Reference to the Farm this inventory item belongs to. (Relationship: Farm 1:N InventoryItem)

name.description: The name of the inventory item (e.g., 'Antibiotic X', 'Boar Semen Batch A', 'Cleaning Solution').

type.description: The category of the inventory item (e.g., medication, semen, supply, other).

unit.description: The unit of measurement for the item (e.g., 'dose', 'bottle', 'kg', 'liter', 'item').

currentStock.description: The current quantity of the item in stock.

minStockLevel.description: The minimum stock level for this item to trigger reorder alerts.

expirationDate.description: The expiration date of the item, if applicable.

purchasePricePerUnit.description: The purchase price of a single unit of this item.

supplier.description: The supplier from whom this item is purchased.

notes.description: General notes or comments about the inventory item.

batchNumber.description: A specific batch number for tracking inventory items (e.g., for medications or semen).

FinancialTransaction.title: FinancialTransaction
FinancialTransaction.description: Records an economic transaction, either an income or an expense, related to farm operations.

id.description: Unique identifier for the FinancialTransaction entity.

farmId.description: Reference to the Farm this transaction occurred on. (Relationship: Farm 1:N FinancialTransaction)

transactionDate.description: The date and time the transaction occurred.

type.description: The type of financial transaction (e.g., income, expense).

category.description: The category of the transaction (e.g., feed, medication, labor, animal sale, facility maintenance, vet services, equipment, utility, other).

amount.description: The monetary amount of the transaction.

description.description: A brief description of the transaction.

relatedEntityId.description: Optional reference to an entity (Sow, Boar, Pig, Batch, Facility, InventoryItem, or specific Event IDs) if the cost/revenue is directly attributable to it. (Relationship: Generic Entity 1:N FinancialTransaction)

recordedByUserId.description: Reference to the User who recorded the financial transaction. (Relationship: User 1:N FinancialTransaction)

notes.description: General notes or comments about the financial transaction.

Subscription.title: Subscription
Subscription.description: Tracks user subscription details for access to SmartPig Lite features.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Subscription entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who holds this subscription. (Relationship: User 1:N Subscription)

plan.description: The subscription plan (e.g., monthly, biannual, annual).

startDate.description: The date when the subscription started.

endDate.description: The date when the subscription is set to end.

status.description: The current status of the subscription (e.g., active, expired, cancelled, pending).

lastPaymentDate.description: The date of the last successful payment.

nextPaymentDate.description: The date of the next scheduled payment.

amountPaid.description: The monetary amount paid for the current subscription period.

currency.description: The currency in which the subscription is paid (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR').

paymentGatewayTransactionId.description: An identifier from the payment gateway for the transaction.

notes.description: General notes or comments about the subscription.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. Each user document contains core identification, authentication details, and a reference to their primary farm (`farmId`). This path leverages path-based ownership for direct user access control. For multi-farm scenarios or roles within a farm, the farm's `members` map is used.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Stores subscription details for a specific user. This subcollection is privately owned by the user, inheriting access control from the parent user document. Access is restricted to `request.auth.uid == userId`.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the subscription.

params.name: subscriptionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the subscription.

definition.description: Top-level collection for farm entities. Each document includes `ownerId` (the UID of the primary owner) and a `members` map (`{uid: role}`) for managing multi-user access and roles (admin, veterinarian, operario). This denormalization ensures Authorization Independence and supports QAPs for listing farms accessible to a user by querying the `members` map.

params.name: farmId
params.description: The unique identifier for the farm.

definition.description: Stores records for individual sows belonging to a specific farm. Includes denormalized `farmId` for path validation, and denormalized `ownerId` and `members` map from the parent farm document for Authorization Independence. Security rules check `request.auth.uid` against these denormalized fields without `get()` calls.

params.name: farmId
params.description: The unique identifier of the farm this sow belongs to.

params.name: sowId
params.description: The unique identifier for the sow.

definition.description: Stores records for individual boars belonging to a specific farm. Includes denormalized `farmId` for path validation, and denormalized `ownerId` and `members` map from the parent farm document for Authorization Independence. Security rules check `request.auth.uid` against these denormalized fields without `get()` calls.

params.name: farmId
params.description: The unique identifier of the farm this boar belongs to.

params.name: boarId
params.description: The unique identifier for the boar.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure for SmartPig Lite prioritizes robust security, scalability, and debuggability by adhering to the core design principles. The primary strategy employed is **Authorization Independence via Denormalization** (Mandate A), which is critical for simplifying security rules and enabling atomic operations.

User-specific data, such as profiles and subscriptions, is organized under `/users/{userId}`. This follows the **Path-Based Ownership** model (Mandate C1), where `request.auth.uid` directly maps to `userId` in the path, making user data inherently private and secure. Subscriptions are nested under users, following **Hierarchical Paths for User-Owned Data** (Mandate C2).

The core of the application revolves around farm management. All farm-related entities are organized as subcollections under a top-level `/farms/{farmId}` collection. This structure leverages Firestore's natural hierarchy for efficient querying of farm-specific data.

To achieve **Authorization Independence (CRITICAL)** and support **QAPs (Rules are not Filters)** for farm-specific data, the following denormalization strategy is applied:
1.  Each document in the `/farms/{farmId}` collection will include `ownerId` (the UID of the farm's primary owner) and a `members` map (e.g., `{ "uid1": "admin", "uid2": "veterinario" }`). This `members` map defines roles for all users associated with that farm, addressing the 'Multiuser management with hierarchical permissions' requirement.
2.  Every document within *all* subcollections of a farm (e.g., `/farms/{farmId}/sows/{sowId}`, `/farms/{farmId}/batches/{batchId}`, etc.) will denormalize both the `farmId` (already present in the schemas) and the `members` map (or at minimum, the `ownerId` and a flag indicating user membership/role) from its parent `Farm` document. This is **CRITICAL** to avoid `get()` calls in security rules. A security rule for a sow document, for instance, can directly check `request.auth.uid in resource.data.members` and `resource.data.farmId == farmId` without needing to fetch the parent `/farms/{farmId}` document.

This denormalization directly enables **QAPs** because `list` operations on subcollections can be performed by clients (e.g., `db.collection('farms').doc(farmId).collection('sows')`), and the security rules will implicitly filter access based on the denormalized `members` field in each document, ensuring only authorized users can read the data. For listing farms, clients can query `db.collection('farms').where('members.' + auth.uid, 'exists', true)`.

**Structural Segregation (Mandate B)** is naturally achieved as each collection (e.g., `sows`, `boars`, `batches`) holds documents of a single entity type, ensuring homogeneous security requirements within that collection. The `status` field in many entities (Sow, Boar, Pig, Facility, Subscription) aligns with **Explicit State Modeling** (Mandate D1). Consistent naming conventions and descriptive wildcards are used throughout to ensure **Data Clarity and Predictability** (Mandate D4).

Regarding the user's reported error `get(ref(db, users/${user.uid}/farmInfo))`: this code snippet appears to be for Firebase Realtime Database. In Firestore, a user's primary farm ID is stored in `/users/{userId}.farmId`. The client should first retrieve this `farmId` from their user profile and then query the actual farm details from `/farms/{user.farmId}`. The structure I've provided supports this corrected Firestore interaction, assuming the client-side code will be updated to use Firestore `doc()` and `getDoc()` methods correctly, following the explicit `farmId` reference in the `User` document.
